# Wallet Coast Customs — AI Themed Mask Overlay for Web3 Wallets

**WCC** is an AI-powered customization studio that paints user-designed themed masks on top of Web3 wallets — without touching wallet logic. The mask is generated by Claude, minted as an NFT on Solana, and rendered by a transparent macOS overlay on top of the real wallet popup. Every keystroke and click passes through to the wallet underneath, so the wallet itself does the actual work (password validation, signing, transactions). We just paint the skin.

> **Status:** demo-stable on Phantom for macOS. The overlay covers the password screen. Other screens and other wallets are aspirational — see [Roadmap](#roadmap).

---

## Why this exists

- Wallets keep all their security-critical logic. We never touch private keys, never modify the wallet's source, never intercept signing.
- The themed mask is purely visual — an Electron transparent window driven by a JSON document.
- Users own the mask as an NFT (theme JSON is stored in the NFT's `theme_data`; background image is on IPFS). Re-applying the theme is one click in the gallery.

## Live demo flow

1. Open [WCC studio on Lovable](https://lovable.dev/projects/9431a51c-2ffc-4f3e-8eb5-115819bf2c10).
2. Describe the theme to the AI assistant ("cyberpunk neon, electric purple", "Linear minimal", "luxury gold"…).
3. Preview the result in the in-app wallet mockup.
4. Mint the theme as a Solana NFT (devnet).
5. Click ⚡ in the gallery — Supabase Realtime broadcasts the mask to the local overlay agent.
6. Open Phantom popup in Chrome — the agent paints the themed mask on top. Type your password, click Unlock; the real Phantom unlocks. After the wallet navigates away from the password screen, the overlay auto-hides.

## Two-repo architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ solana-style-studio (THIS REPO, Lovable hosted)             │
│ github.com/Alexanfr-God/solana-style-studio                 │
│                                                             │
│   • WCC web app + AI Theme Assistant                        │
│   • src/utils/wccToLayoutDocument.ts                        │
│       assembles the broadcast JSON per anchor               │
│   • src/stores/phantomThemeStore.ts                         │
│       WCCOverlayV3 theme → per-element CSS                  │
│   • supabase/functions/theme-orchestrator/                  │
│       Claude Sonnet 4.5 multi-agent pipeline:               │
│       design-system-selector → background-agent →           │
│       color-analyst → designer → element-designer →         │
│       director                                              │
│       + 14 curated DESIGN.md system prompts                 │
│   • DynamicPhantomRenderer — in-app wallet mockup           │
│   • NFT mint + Minted Gallery                               │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                           │  Supabase Realtime
                           │  channel "wcc:overlay"
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ phantom-overlay-editor (sibling repo, runs on user's Mac)   │
│ github.com/Alexanfr-God/phantom-overlay-editor              │
│                                                             │
│   • packages/sync-server — Realtime → ws://:3333 bridge     │
│   • packages/agent       — Electron + Swift CGEvent overlay │
│   • packages/desktop     — admin window (opacity slider…)   │
│   • packages/shared      — Zod WS message schemas           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick start — web side (this repo)

```bash
git clone git@github.com:Alexanfr-God/solana-style-studio.git
cd solana-style-studio
npm install

# Configure Supabase (only VITE_SUPABASE_URL + VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY required)
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env

npm run dev          # web app at http://localhost:8080
```

Theme generation calls the `theme-orchestrator` Edge Function, which is already deployed to our shared Supabase project. If you want to run your own backend, see the Edge Function setup below.

## Quick start — overlay side

The macOS overlay agent lives in a separate repo. From a second terminal:

```bash
git clone git@github.com:Alexanfr-God/phantom-overlay-editor.git
cd phantom-overlay-editor
npm install
npm run dev:server    # sync-server :3333
npm run dev:agent     # macOS overlay (Swift compile ~30-60s first time)
npm run dev:desktop   # admin window with opacity slider
```

Open Phantom popup → click ⚡ in WCC → mask appears on top. Full instructions, prerequisites, and accessibility-permission gotcha: [`phantom-overlay-editor/README.md`](https://github.com/Alexanfr-God/phantom-overlay-editor#readme).

## Edge Function setup (if running your own backend)

```bash
# Install Supabase CLI (one-time)
brew install supabase/tap/supabase

# Set the Anthropic key
SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=<your-token> \
  supabase secrets set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... \
  --project-ref <your-project-ref>

# Deploy the orchestrator
SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=<your-token> \
  supabase functions deploy theme-orchestrator \
  --project-ref <your-project-ref>
```

The orchestrator expects exactly `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (uppercase, no spaces) — Supabase env vars are picky about names.

## What's in this repo (notable files)

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/pages/Documentation.tsx` | In-app docs (About / Roadmap / Architecture / AI Model / etc.) |
| `src/utils/wccToLayoutDocument.ts` | Builds the broadcast layout document (PASSWORD_ANCHORS = element positions + types) |
| `src/stores/phantomThemeStore.ts` | Converts `WCCOverlayV3` theme JSON to per-anchor CSS overrides |
| `src/components/wallet/DynamicPhantomRenderer.tsx` | Renders the mockup wallet preview |
| `supabase/functions/theme-orchestrator/index.ts` | Orchestration glue |
| `supabase/functions/theme-orchestrator/agents/` | Multi-agent pipeline (Claude calls) |
| `supabase/functions/theme-orchestrator/design-systems/` | 14 `DESIGN.md` files: linear, stripe, vercel, coinbase, robinhood, cash-app, apple, notion, raycast, elevenlabs, cyberpunk, vaporwave, luxury-gold, dark-minimal |
| `public/phantom-ghost.svg` | Canonical Phantom-style ghost logo (referenced by GitHub raw URL in broadcasts) |
| `public/phantom-layout.json` | Static layout file used by the mockup renderer |

## Tech stack

- **Frontend:** React + TypeScript + Tailwind + Zustand
- **Backend:** Supabase Postgres + Edge Functions (Deno) + Realtime + Storage
- **AI:** Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.5 (`claude-sonnet-4-5`) via the Messages API
- **On-chain:** Solana web3.js + Metaplex (NFT mint) + IPFS via Lighthouse
- **Overlay:** Electron + Swift CoreGraphics (the agent — sibling repo)

## What is NOT yet integrated (transparency)

- **Real Phantom Chrome extension:** we overlay on top of the popup with a transparent window; we don't modify Phantom's source. Phantom can't tell our overlay exists.
- **Wallet partnerships:** no wallet team has signed off on theme masks. Future integrations depend on wallet developers choosing to support an overlay protocol.
- **Other wallets:** MetaMask, Solflare, Backpack, etc. are out of scope for the current demo. Architecture is wallet-agnostic in principle but only Phantom is wired up.
- **Mainnet mint:** currently devnet only.
- **Platform other than macOS:** the overlay agent uses Swift CGEvent; Windows/Linux would need a parallel native implementation.
